title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Main
The freight market continues to lose momentum, while the number of vessels sailing in ballast status has gradually increased in the first days of April. It seems that the growth of demand ton-days has decreased since the first half of March, triggering downward pressure on the spot freight rates. In the iron ore market, cargo prices with a 63.5% iron content for delivery into Tianjin have suddenly risen above $150/t, a price not seen in the last three weeks.

children.title: Main
children.summary: The freight market continues to lose momentum, while the number of vessels sailing in ballast status has gradually increased in the first days of April. It seems that the growth of demand ton-days has decreased since the first half of March, triggering downward pressure on the spot freight rates.

children.title: SECTION 1 - FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) - Weaker
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
Image reference: ch1.png | assets/2022-04-06_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch1_2429d6dad9c4.png
A weaker sentiment of freight rates is mainly reflected in the smaller vessel size categories for this week, while freight rates for Capesize vessels are firmed up. Capesize Brazil-to-North China freight rates increased above $26/t, while the past peak was Week 50, 2021 at levels of around $50/t.

children.title: Number of Vessels - Increasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: ch2.png | assets/2022-04-06_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch2_2429d6dad9c4.png
The uptick of ballasters signals an increasing trend for all vessel size categories for the first days of April, however, the number of ballasters for the bigger vessels has grown at a comparatively slower pace. Capesize SE Africa: The number of vessels sailing in ballast increased slightly this week to around 72 vessels, compared to around 69 during the last days of March.

children.title: Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: ch3.png | assets/2022-04-06_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch3_a4131d3b415d.png
The overall trend of demand ton-days is decreasing, while the accelerated growth evidenced in the Panamax segment during the previous two weeks seems now to have paused. Capesize demand ton-days: There is a sharp dropdown in the first days of April following the last peak during the previous week.

children.title: Dry bulk ships congested around Chinese ports
section_path: Dry bulk ships congested around Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: Dry bulk ships congested around Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: ch4.png | assets/2022-04-06_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch4_56fe7bb4fba1.png
Dry bulk ships in congestion have shown a slight decrease for the first days of April, but still higher than the lows within Weeks 8, 9, and 10. The downward trend is mainly due to the drop in the supramax segment.
